task: Write a Python function second_largest(nums) returning the second largest distinct value in the list, or None when there are fewer than two distinct values. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The provided function correctly identifies the second largest distinct value in a list, and handles an edge case where there are fewer than two distinct values.
trace: The function first removes any duplicates in the list by converting it into a set. Then, it sorts the unique numbers in reverse order (from greatest to smallest). If there are less than two distinct values in the list, the function returns None to indicate no second largest value exists. Otherwise, it returns the second highest number from the sorted list. This approach ensures that even if duplicates of the maximum number exist, the function still finds and returns the next largest unique value.
